corpus.title: Thriving Economy Pillar — Richmond Civic Hackathon
corpus.description: Research corpus on Richmond's economic opportunity problem space: MBE contracting access, small business formation navigation, and workforce pathways.

files.title: Thriving Economy Pillar — Main Entry Point
files.summary: Primary entry point for hackathon teams. Covers the three core questions, decision phases (0–9), guardrails, repo map, and Hackbot introduction. Read first for orientation.

files.title: Quick Start — 6-Step Minimal Path
files.summary: Six-step minimal path from landing to MVP scope. Prioritized for teams with 30 minutes or less before they need to make decisions.
recommended_audience: teams that need a clear starting point

files.title: Hackbot Agent Specification
files.summary: Full specification for Hackbot: personality, boot sequence, skill system, research corpus navigation instructions, guardrails, and decision logic. AI agents should read this before acting.

files.title: Research Corpus Guide
files.summary: Self-describing guide for humans and AI agents on how to navigate and use the research corpus. Covers canonical source of truth hierarchy, section structure, navigation decision tree, and agent behavior requirements.
recommended_audience: hackathon participants unfamiliar with the corpus

files.title: Maintenance Guide
files.summary: Instructions for adding, updating, and synchronizing research reports, metadata, indexes, and navigation files. Required reading before modifying the corpus structure.
recommended_audience: AI agents tasked with corpus updates

files.title: Hackbot Boot Prompt
files.summary: Initialization prompt for Hackbot. Contains the condensed boot sequence and initial framing for AI agent sessions.

files.title: Claude Role Definition
files.summary: Role and operating rules for Claude AI in this repository: civic research guide, Socratic coach, MVP advisor. Defines what Claude should and should not do, preferred and weak solution patterns, and the default task sequence.

files.title: Pillar Overview
files.summary: Overview of the Thriving Economy pillar problem space: equitable contracting access, small business navigation, workforce pathways, and entrepreneurship support. Foundational context for all research.

files.title: MAP Alignment
files.summary: Alignment of this pillar with the Mayor's Action Plan (MAP) Pillars 4 and 7. Explains the civic and strategic context for hackathon solutions.

files.title: Blue-Sky Problem Statements
files.summary: Broad, unconstrained problem statements for exploratory ideation. Use for teams still in orientation and wanting a wide problem canvas before narrowing.

files.title: Targeted Problem Statements (Scored)
files.summary: Two scored, rubric-evaluated problem statements ready for hackathon selection: MBE Contracting (26/32) and Small Business Navigation (24/32). Includes rubric dimensions: clarity, scope, data readiness, champion, population impact, operating environment, success criteria, accessibility.

files.title: Data Index
files.summary: Index of available data sources for the Thriving Economy pillar. Points to the source inventory CSV and explains data categories.

files.title: Source Inventory (CSV)
files.summary: Structured inventory of public data sources with access mode, key fields, and notes. Primary reference for data availability. Includes Socrata API details for Richmond contracts data.

files.title: Research Notes — Executive Brief
files.summary: Primary synthesized artifact: executive brief plus full research synthesis promoted from verified evidence. The best single document for teams needing comprehensive findings quickly. 21+ KB of findings.
recommended_audience: anyone needing the executive summary of all research

files.title: Prototype Recommendations
files.summary: Three seeded MVP concepts with rationale: Procurement Opportunity Translator, Small Business Setup Navigator, Trades Career Explorer. Includes recommended starting direction.

files.title: User Journeys
files.summary: Structured journey maps for primary user personas: MBE contractor seeking city contracts, small business owner navigating formation. Useful for MVP design and demo narrative.

files.title: Small Business Decision Tree
files.summary: Decision tree for the business formation sequence in Richmond, covering the order of steps for registration, licensing, and compliance. Directly usable as prototype content.

files.title: MBE Data Feasibility Assessment
files.summary: Assessment of data readiness for an MBE-focused prototype: what public data exists, what is accessible via API, what gaps exist. Grounded in D section research.

files.title: Benchmark Scan
files.summary: Scan of comparable civic tech tools from other jurisdictions. Synthesizes E section findings on prior art: what has been built, what patterns transfer, what has failed.

files.title: Continuation Plan
files.summary: Post-hackathon pathways for continuing work: potential partners, institutions, and next steps for each prototype direction.

files.title: MVP Shapes
files.summary: Five MVP archetype patterns (A–E) for Thriving Economy projects. Each shape describes user, core function, data sources, tech stack sketches, and scope boundary.

files.title: Recommended Architectures
files.summary: Technical architecture sketches for each MVP shape. Includes stack recommendations, data pipeline patterns, and AI integration constraints.

files.title: Demo Advice
files.summary: Structure for a 90-second demo: problem, user, flow, sources, limits, next steps. Aligned with judging criteria for civic tech hackathons.

files.title: Deep Research Report — Evidence Synthesis
files.summary: Evidence-based synthesis of the full research corpus. 20.5 KB. The authoritative synthesized view of all findings with source citations. Distinct from research_notes.md in that it has stronger citation tracing.
recommended_audience: researchers needing the most thorough synthesis

files.title: Evidence Log
files.summary: Verified claims table with 10 entries (E-001 through E-010). Each entry has: official source URL, access date (2026-03-15), verification status (Confirmed/Likely/Missing), and notes. Use to check specific factual claims.

files.title: Skill: repo_memory
files.summary: Reconstruct repository state from artifacts. Read project files, detect team stage, summarize progress. Used in boot sequence.

files.title: Skill: problem_scoping
files.summary: Help teams choose a strong problem: analyze problem statements, rank opportunities, identify 3 realistic directions.

files.title: Skill: research_runner
files.summary: Execute research prompts, extract sources, and summarize evidence. Used in Phase 3 research spin-up.

files.title: Skill: dataset_mapper
files.summary: Scan data directory, identify schemas and formats, highlight gaps. Used to answer 'what data is available?' questions.

files.title: Skill: opportunity_mapper
files.summary: Match problems to solution patterns and suggest MVP directions. Used in Phase 4 opportunity framing.

files.title: Skill: mvp_designer
files.summary: Turn an opportunity into a concrete MVP: user flow, architecture, scope boundaries. Used in Phase 5 scope lock.

files.title: Skill: risk_review
files.summary: Prevent civic-tech mistakes: check for hallucinated services, legal claims, eligibility logic errors, and misleading language.

files.title: Skill: demo_coach
files.summary: Help teams present effectively: demo script, narrative structure, judge-facing explanation. Used in Phase 8–9.

files.title: Skill: repo_librarian
files.summary: Navigate repository knowledge quickly: answer 'where is X?' questions, return relevant file paths with descriptions.

files.title: Skill: continuity_planner
files.summary: Post-hackathon continuation pathways: potential partners, artifacts worth continuing, next steps.

files.title: Skill: research_corpus_navigation
files.summary: Map a user question to the correct section and specific research reports before answering. Uses research/index.json and section mappings to build a targeted reading list.

files.title: Skill: cross_report_synthesis
files.summary: Synthesize findings from multiple research reports with source traceability, explicit tension identification, and gap documentation.
recommended_audience: AI agents answering complex multi-report questions

files.title: Skill: evidence_grounded_answering
files.summary: Answer questions using only content present in the corpus. Enforces claim classification (confirmed, stated, uncertain, not found) and blocks fabrication.

files.title: Skill: report_update_protocol
files.summary: Safely add or update research reports and keep all metadata, indexes, and navigation files synchronized. Step-by-step protocol with validation checklist.
recommended_audience: AI agents tasked with adding reports

files.title: Deep Research Corpus (51 reports)
files.summary: 51 deep research reports generated via Perplexity sonar-deep-research covering problem landscape, users, services, data, prior art, opportunities, risks, MVP shapes, and demo strategy. Each report exists as .md and .json. Full metadata in research/index.json.
files.note: See research/index.json for individual file entries. See research/README.md for human-readable table of contents.

files.title: Research Index (machine-readable)
files.summary: JSON array with one entry per research report: id, section, title, 1-2 sentence summary, 6 key_terms. The primary retrieval index for the research corpus. Load this before deciding which research files to read.

files.title: Research Directory Index (human-readable)
files.summary: Human-readable table of contents for all 51 research reports, organized by section (Context, Cross-Cutting, A–I) with one-line descriptions.

files.title: Project One-Pager Template
files.summary: Template for scoping an MVP: must-haves, mockables, data sources, AI role, limits, and demo path. Use for Phase 5 scope lock.

files.title: Team Profile Template
files.summary: YAML/Markdown template for team profile: name, decision maker, members with roles/skills/availability, communication preferences, constraints. Required by Hackbot boot sequence.

files.title: Decision Memo Template
files.summary: Short template for recording problem selection rationale: chosen problem, user, why this one, why not others, risks.

files.title: Working Direction Note Template
files.summary: 5-bullet quick-capture template for initial problem framing: candidate problem, likely user, why it matters, why it is weekend-buildable, biggest uncertainty.
